title: Set up LangChain RAG pipeline in a Next.js project
summary: Complete LangChain.js setup for Next.js — document ingestion, embeddings, vector store (Pinecone or in-memory), LCEL retrieval chain, and streaming responses via the Vercel AI SDK.
declaration: I will set up a LangChain RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) pipeline in this Next.js project. This includes installing LangChain.js, configuring an LLM and embeddings model, setting up a vector store, building a retrieval chain, and exposing a streaming API route. Let me know which vector store you want to use (Pinecone, Supabase pgvector, or in-memory for development) and which LLM provider (OpenAI or Anthropic) before I begin.
instructions:
## Set Up LangChain RAG Pipeline in a Next.js Project ### 1. Pre-flight checks - Verify `package.json` exists and the project is a Next.js 13+ App Router project. - Check if LangChain packages are already installed: look for `langchain` or `@langchain/core` in dependencies. If found, skip to step 4. - Determine the package manager in use (pnpm/yarn/bun/npm). - Clarify with the user: - **LLM provider**: OpenAI (default) or Anthropic Claude. - **Vector store**: Pinecone (production), Supabase pgvector (if already using Supabase), or MemoryVectorStore (development/testing only). - **Document source**: PDF files, plain text, website URLs, or existing database records. ### 2. Install dependencies - Install LangChain core and the chosen provider: ``` npm install langchain @langchain/core @langchain/openai ``` For Anthropic instead: ``` npm install langchain @langchain/core @langchain/anthropic ``` - Install the vector store integration (choose one): - **Pinecone**: `npm install @langchain/pinecone @pinecone-database/pinecone` - **Supabase pgvector**: `npm install @langchain/community` (includes Supabase vector store) - **In-memory** (dev only): included in `langchain` — no extra install. - Install document loaders and text splitters: ``` npm install pdf-parse ``` ### 3. Configure environment variables - Add to `.env.local`: ``` # LLM Provider (choose one) OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-... # ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... # Vector store (Pinecone example) PINECONE_API_KEY=your_pinecone_api_key PINECONE_INDEX=your_index_name # Optional: LangSmith tracing (highly recommended for debugging) LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true LANGCHAIN_API_KEY=your_langsmith_api_key LANGCHAIN_PROJECT=my-nextjs-rag ``` - Add all keys to `.env.example` with placeholder values. - `LANGCHAIN_TRACING_V2=true` enables LangSmith — it traces every chain step visually. Strongly recommended during development. ### 4. Create the LLM and embeddings clients - Create `src/lib/langchain.ts`: ```typescript import { ChatOpenAI, OpenAIEmbeddings } from '@langchain/openai' export const llm = new ChatOpenAI({ model: 'gpt-4o-mini', temperature: 0, streaming: true, }) export const embeddings = new OpenAIEmbeddings({ model: 'text-embedding-3-small', }) ``` For Anthropic: ```typescript import { ChatAnthropic } from '@langchain/anthropic' export const llm = new ChatAnthropic({ model: 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022', temperature: 0, streaming: true, }) ``` - `temperature: 0` reduces hallucination in factual RAG responses — increase for creative tasks. ### 5. Set up the vector store **Option A — Pinecone (production recommended):** - Create the index in the Pinecone dashboard: dimension `1536` (for `text-embedding-3-small`) or `3072` (for `text-embedding-3-large`). Metric: `cosine`. - Create `src/lib/vectorstore.ts`: ```typescript import { PineconeStore } from '@langchain/pinecone' import { Pinecone } from '@pinecone-database/pinecone' import { embeddings } from './langchain' const pinecone = new Pinecone({ apiKey: process.env.PINECONE_API_KEY! }) export async function getVectorStore() { const index = pinecone.Index(process.env.PINECONE_INDEX!) return PineconeStore.fromExistingIndex(embeddings, { pineconeIndex: index }) } ``` **Option B — In-memory (development only):** ```typescript import { MemoryVectorStore } from 'langchain/vectorstores/memory' import { embeddings } from './langchain' let store: MemoryVectorStore | null = null export async function getVectorStore() { if (!store) { store = new MemoryVectorStore(embeddings) } return store } ``` Warn the user: MemoryVectorStore resets on every server restart — documents must be re-ingested each time. Use only for local testing. ### 6. Build the document ingestion pipeline - Create `src/lib/ingest.ts`: ```typescript import { PDFLoader } from 'langchain/document_loaders/fs/pdf' import { RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter } from 'langchain/text_splitter' import { getVectorStore } from './vectorstore' export async function ingestPDF(filePath: string) { const loader = new PDFLoader(filePath) const docs = await loader.load() const splitter = new RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter({ chunkSize: 1000, chunkOverlap: 200, }) const chunks = await splitter.splitDocuments(docs) const store = await getVectorStore() await store.addDocuments(chunks) console.log(`Ingested ${chunks.length} chunks`) } ``` - `chunkSize: 1000` / `chunkOverlap: 200` are sensible defaults. Reduce `chunkSize` for precise retrieval; increase for more context per chunk. - Run ingestion once (or on document update) via a separate script: `npx tsx src/scripts/ingest.ts`. - For website URLs, use `CheerioWebBaseLoader` instead of `PDFLoader`. ### 7. Build the RAG retrieval chain (LCEL) - Create `src/lib/rag-chain.ts`: ```typescript import { ChatPromptTemplate } from '@langchain/core/prompts' import { StringOutputParser } from '@langchain/core/output_parsers' import { RunnableSequence, RunnablePassthrough } from '@langchain/core/runnables' import { formatDocumentsAsString } from 'langchain/util/document' import { llm } from './langchain' import { getVectorStore } from './vectorstore' const prompt = ChatPromptTemplate.fromTemplate(` You are an assistant. Answer based only on the provided context. If the context does not contain the answer, say "I don't have information about that." Context: {context} Question: {question} `) export async function buildRAGChain() { const store = await getVectorStore() const retriever = store.asRetriever({ k: 4 }) return RunnableSequence.from([ { context: retriever.pipe(formatDocumentsAsString), question: new RunnablePassthrough(), }, prompt, llm, new StringOutputParser(), ]) } ``` - `k: 4` retrieves the 4 most relevant chunks. Increase for more context; decrease for faster, cheaper responses. - The prompt explicitly instructs the model not to hallucinate beyond the context — critical for RAG quality. ### 8. Create the streaming API route - Create `app/api/chat/route.ts`: ```typescript import { StreamingTextResponse, LangChainStream } from 'ai' import { buildRAGChain } from '@/lib/rag-chain' export const runtime = 'nodejs' export async function POST(req: Request) { const { messages } = await req.json() const question = messages[messages.length - 1].content const { stream, handlers } = LangChainStream() const chain = await buildRAGChain() chain.invoke(question, { callbacks: [handlers] }) return new StreamingTextResponse(stream) } ``` - `LangChainStream` from the Vercel AI SDK bridges LangChain streaming to the `StreamingTextResponse` format. - `export const runtime = 'nodejs'` is required — LangChain does not support the Edge Runtime due to Node.js API usage. - Do NOT await `chain.invoke` — fire it without await so the stream starts immediately. ### 9. Create the chat UI - Install the Vercel AI SDK hook: ``` npm install ai ``` - Create `app/chat/page.tsx`: ```tsx 'use client' import { useChat } from 'ai/react' export default function ChatPage() { const { messages, input, handleInputChange, handleSubmit, isLoading } = useChat({ api: '/api/chat', }) return (
{messages.map((m) => (
{m.role}: {m.content}
))}
) } ``` ### 10. Verify the setup - Run ingestion: `npx tsx src/scripts/ingest.ts` (create a simple script that calls `ingestPDF`). - Run `npm run dev`, open `http://localhost:3000/chat`, and ask a question about the ingested document. - If LangSmith is configured, check `smith.langchain.com` — each chain invocation is traced step-by-step. ### Common errors & fixes - **`Cannot use import statement in a module` for `pdf-parse`** → Add to `next.config.ts`: ```js const nextConfig = { serverExternalPackages: ['pdf-parse'] } ``` - **Pinecone 404 index not found** → The index name in `PINECONE_INDEX` must exactly match (case-sensitive) the index created in the Pinecone dashboard. - **Pinecone dimension mismatch** → The index dimension must match the embedding model: `text-embedding-3-small` → 1536, `text-embedding-ada-002` → 1536, `text-embedding-3-large` → 3072. Deleting and recreating the index is the only fix. - **Edge Runtime error: `require is not defined`** → LangChain requires Node.js. Add `export const runtime = 'nodejs'` to every API route that uses LangChain. - **`StreamingTextResponse` is not exported from 'ai'** → Vercel AI SDK v3+ changed the API. Use `import { streamText } from 'ai'` with LangChain's `.stream()` method instead of `LangChainStream`. - **RAG returns irrelevant chunks** → Increase `k`, reduce `chunkSize`, or add metadata filtering to the retriever. Check the LangSmith trace to see which chunks were retrieved. - **Slow first response** → `getVectorStore()` is initialized on every request. Cache the vector store instance in a module-level variable (same singleton pattern as PrismaClient). ### What NOT to do - Do NOT use the Edge Runtime (`export const runtime = 'edge'`) with LangChain — it relies on Node.js built-ins and will fail at runtime. - Do NOT store API keys in client components or expose `OPENAI_API_KEY` via `NEXT_PUBLIC_` — these are server secrets. - Do NOT skip document chunking and embed entire files — LLM context windows and vector similarity both work best with small, focused chunks. - Do NOT use `MemoryVectorStore` in production — it loses all data on restart and does not scale. - Do NOT call `buildRAGChain()` without caching the result per request — building the chain on every message is redundant but acceptable; re-initializing the vector store connection on every request is expensive. - Do NOT ignore LangSmith during development — debugging a broken chain without traces is extremely difficult.
